title: Playground Devbench
concept: A local-development launcher for theme authors that turns Playground into a tabbed run bench: route-specific PR, GitHub, Blueprint, and zip starts are first-class, while save state, manager tools, catalog inspection, and export results remain visible as workflow outputs.
focus: tabbed workspace instead of a three-pane console
dark diagnostics surface with light content cards
flows: Choose a distinct launch route for Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or zip import with real inputs and consequences.
Save an unsaved Playground to browser storage or to a local directory with separate destination behavior, copy progress, and resulting saved identity.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with rename, delete, open, temporary-state warnings, and browser-storage consequences.
Use Site Manager controls for settings reset/reload, file browser editing, Blueprint JSON actions, database inspection, and logs.
Browse a dense Blueprint catalog with category filters, search, selected Blueprint detail, run, inspect, and copy behavior.
Export and import through GitHub, zip download/import, Blueprint bundle download, and database.sqlite download.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
